sources: General sports nutrition consensus — no single position stand; reflects ACSM and AND practical guidance
note: Meal prep is a practical adherence strategy, not a nutrition intervention. Its value is making the default choice the right one.
core_principles: Prep protein sources first — they take the longest and are the hardest to improvise on busy nights
Batch cook neutral bases (rice, quinoa, oats) that work across multiple meals
Pre-cut and wash vegetables so they're grab-and-go during the week
Prepare 3–4 days at a time, not 7 — fresh food after day 4–5 is noticeably less appealing

protein_batch.use_for: Lunch over rice, dinner with roti, breakfast with eggs
protein_batch.tip: Cook a large pot, portion into containers. Freeze half if making for the week.

protein_batch.use_for: Lunch bowl, wrap filling, side with quinoa
protein_batch.tip: Canned chickpeas halve the prep time with no nutritional trade-off.

protein_batch.use_for: Stir-fry, salad topping, grain bowl protein
protein_batch.tip: Press tofu for 15 min before cooking — removes moisture so it crisps instead of steaming.

protein_batch.use_for: Snack, grain bowl add-on, high-protein side

carb_batch.tip: Cook 2 cups dry — yields 5+ portions. Portion into containers while hot.
Complete protein — doubles as carb and protein source. Toast dry before adding water for nutty flavour.
Pre-portion 40g servings into jars with seeds and dried fruit — overnight oats ready in 3 minutes.

vegetable_prep: Wash and chop a week's worth of spinach, broccoli, zucchini, bell pepper
Roast a large tray of root vegetables (sweet potato, carrot, beet) — eat hot or cold
Keep a bag of washed cucumber and carrot sticks for snacking

quick_assembly_meals.note: These combine prepped components and take under 5 minutes.

examples.name: Overnight oats
components: add milk (LF) + banana the night before

storage_safety.fridge: Cooked food safe for 4–5 days at <4°C (40°F).
storage_safety.freezer: Dal, curry, and cooked grains freeze well for up to 3 months.
storage_safety.label: Always label with date. First-in, first-out.
storage_safety.reheat: Reheat to steaming hot (>74°C / 165°F) all the way through.

30_min_sunday_plan.description: A realistic 30-minute prep session that sets up 4 days of lunches and dinners.
steps: Start rice or quinoa (takes 20–35 min unattended)
While grains cook: sauté onion + tomato base for dal (5 min), add lentils + spices + water
While dal simmers: wash and chop vegetables, store in containers
While dal finishes: portion oats into jars with seeds for breakfast
Total active time: ~25 min. Hands-off time: ~20 min.
